description: §6.6.9 escape hatch #1 (RULING A) — passing the client value as an EXPLICIT ARGUMENT is clean. `saveOrder(total)` reads the bare parameter `total` (never `@total`), and the call site `saveOrder(@total)` evaluates `@total` client-side and passes it as an argument — function arguments ARE marshalled into the request body, so the value is transported. No E-REACTIVE-003. This is the corrected form of the server-fn-derived-read-error case. Asserts the whole family stays absent.
rationale: §6.6.9 fix (1): 'Pass the value as an explicit argument. Function arguments ARE marshalled into the request body. Give the function a parameter and read the parameter (a bare name, not @cell); the caller passes the client cell at the call site.'
